response.justification: Candidate brings valuable geospatial platform experience (GISCollective) and distributed systems exposure (Dunnhumby microservices, Kubernetes), plus demonstrated polyglot adaptability. However, C++ skills are over a decade stale with no modern C++ or Rust experience—critical for a modern routing pipeline. More significantly, there is zero navigation or routing systems experience despite the GIS background, making the domain-specific gap substantial. Would require heavy ramp-up on both language modernization and routing/navigation fundamentals.
strengths: Built GISCollective, a production geospatial data platform—directly relevant domain knowledge and proven ability to ship geospatial systems
Distributed systems exposure: microservices architecture at Dunnhumby, Kubernetes deployment with GISCollective, handling scale and reliability concerns
Polyglot engineer across C++, D Lang, JavaScript, Java—proven ability to learn new stacks and adapt to technical requirements
concerns: C++ experience ended in 2015 (11 years ago); current work is TypeScript/Node.js web development, not systems-level programming
Zero Rust experience and no modern C++ portfolio, despite job requiring either modern C++ or Rust as primary skill
No navigation systems, routing algorithms, or tile-generation experience despite geospatial background—lacks domain-specific systems knowledge

key_factors.factor: Outdated C++ (2015) with no modern systems work
No navigation or routing systems experience
